description: Test cases for the marketing-plan skill v2. Covers happy paths, edge cases, and negative triggers. Updated to assert competitive landscape, budget allocation, messaging, journey map, retention campaigns, and measurement setup.

cases.name: SaaS product — skill-as-a-service platform
cases.prompt: Build a marketing plan for a new SaaS platform that lets developers deploy AI skills as paid APIs. Product is fully online, bootstrapped, pre-launch, targeting US developers first. No existing customers.
assertions: Output file named marketing-plan-*.md is created in the working directory
Output contains section '## 2. Target Market' with a JTBD statement
Output contains section '## 3. Competitive Landscape' with a terrain map table
Output contains section '## 4. Positioning + Messaging' with a positioning one-liner AND primary message + supporting messages
Output contains section '## 5. Channel Strategy' with a buyer journey map table AND primary, supporting, and retention channels
Output contains 'GEV' or 'Generative Engine' in the channel strategy section
Output contains section '## 6. Budget Allocation' with a spend allocation table
Output contains section '## 8. Content Strategy' with retention email campaigns (welcome series, activation nudge, win-back)
Output contains section '## 9. 90-Day Launch Timeline' with Phase 0, Phase 1, Phase 2
Output contains section '## 12. KPI Framework' with AARRR metrics table AND pre-launch analytics checklist
Skill presented 3 ICP options before proceeding (Gate A fired)
Skill presented 3 positioning directions before proceeding (Gate B fired)
Skill presented 3 channel mixes before proceeding (Gate C fired)

cases.name: DTC ecommerce — home goods brand
cases.prompt: Create a marketing plan for a dropship home goods DTC brand called True Norma. Selling minimalist Nordic-style kitchen and living accessories. Online only, bootstrapped, pre-launch, US market. No customers yet.
assertions: Output file named marketing-plan-*.md is created
Output contains DTC-appropriate channels (Meta, TikTok, or Instagram mentioned)
Output references email list or owned channel as retention lever
Output contains competitive landscape section with at least 3 competitors mapped
Output contains budget allocation with $0 or minimal paid spend (bootstrapped)
Output contains 90-day launch timeline with Phase 0 pre-launch validation
Output contains ROAS or CAC or LTV in KPI section
Output includes GEV or AI search tactic
Phase 0 mentions inventory confirmation or supplier validation
Output contains retention email campaigns (welcome series at minimum)
Output contains messaging section with channel-specific hooks

cases.name: Service business — fractional CMO
cases.prompt: Write a marketing plan for a new fractional CMO service targeting early-stage B2B SaaS founders. Online delivery, bootstrapped, 2 pilot clients, US market.
assertions: Output file named marketing-plan-*.md is created
Output references LinkedIn as a primary or supporting channel
Output includes direct outreach or referral mechanism
Output contains positioning one-liner AND primary message
Output includes revenue per client or LTV as a KPI
Output includes case studies or testimonials as content type
Output contains competitive landscape with named alternatives
Output contains budget allocation appropriate for bootstrapped service business

cases.name: SaaS with existing personal brand — flywheel modifier
cases.prompt: Build a marketing plan for a new online course platform teaching AI engineering skills. I have a LinkedIn following of 5,000 developers who engage regularly with my posts about AI tools. Product is $49/month subscription, bootstrapped, pre-launch, global English-speaking market.
assertions: Output identifies personal brand flywheel as active
Output uses personal brand as primary or top channel (not just supporting)
Output channel strategy references warm audience activation, not cold outreach
Output budget allocation shifts spend toward content production and email, away from paid ads
Output first customer motion references activating existing audience, not cold seeding
Output contains competitive landscape section

cases.name: Edge case — pre-launch, zero budget, unknown ICP
cases.prompt: Build a marketing plan for a new online marketplace connecting independent artists with buyers. Pre-launch, bootstrapped with zero marketing budget, not sure who the primary ICP is yet (artists or buyers?).
assertions: Skill identifies the cold-start / two-sided marketplace challenge
Skill recommends resolving supply vs demand side sequencing
Channel recommendations are zero-cost or organic only (no paid ads recommended for bootstrapped zero budget)
Budget allocation shows $0 paid spend with focus on founder time allocation
Output flags ICP ambiguity and prompts user to choose artist vs buyer as primary ICP before proceeding
Output file created with plan after ICP is clarified

cases.name: Edge case — existing context file present
cases.prompt: Build a marketing plan for our DTC store. [Assume product-marketing-context.md exists in working directory with product details pre-filled]
assertions: Skill checks for existing context file before asking all 7 intake questions
Skill pre-fills answers from context file and only asks for missing information
Output plan reflects information from the context file

cases.name: Negative trigger — individual content piece request
cases.prompt: Write me a welcome email sequence for new customers of my SaaS product.
assertions: Skill does NOT activate — this is a content creation task, not a marketing plan task
Response directs user to a content creation skill or handles the task directly without invoking marketing-plan process

cases.name: Negative trigger — existing marketing audit
cases.prompt: Audit my current marketing strategy and tell me what's not working. We've been running Facebook ads for 6 months with declining ROAS.
assertions: Skill does NOT activate — this is an audit of existing marketing, not a plan for a new business
Response addresses the audit question directly without running the marketing-plan GTM process
